review_questions: Does CP00-152 cover exactly the 40 planned Risk B evidence workflow and fixture units in order?
Can any CP00-152 evidence workflow row evaluate permission/audit binding, emit receipts, write fixtures, execute tests, or expose hidden internals?
Can any CP00-152 fixture evidence row execute Hermes commands, emit receipts, materialize evidence templates, send Claude prompts, record human markers, or write audit/product state?
Are H03/C03 packet descriptors and PASS/PASS_WITH_FINDINGS/BLOCK semantics reference-only and non-authoritative?
Does CP00-152 hand off cleanly from CP00-151 to CP00-153 without implementing LDIP or splitting HRX?

parsed_review.summary: CP00-152 is a Risk B no-write descriptor pack binding RP03.P08.M04.S20-RP03.P08.M06.S17 (40 units: 1 secondary-workflow terminal row in M04, 22 permission/audit evidence-binding rows in M05, 17 synthetic-fixture opening rows in M06). Coverage is correct and contiguous: generated unit IDs (M04.S20; M05.S01-S22; M06.S01-S17) yield first=RP03.P08.M04.S20, last=RP03.P08.M06.S17, 40 unique IDs, and validateAuditComplianceCp152Coverage compares element-by-element against the plan's included_units (validation passed). Deliverable/evidence-mode distributions (hermes_evidence:20, implementation:17, claude_review:2, test:1; terminal:1/binding:22/fixture:17) match both the readiness object and the architecture validator. No-write semantics are exhaustively enforced via CP152_NO_WRITE_ATTESTATION and freezeNoWriteResult and asserted across all 40 catalog rows plus targeted cases; the catalog performs no audit/product writes, no permission/audit evaluation, no Hermes command execution, no fixture materialization, no Claude prompt send, and declares implements_ldip/splits_hrx_product false with no cross-tenant count/object-name exposure. Contract schema bumped v0.18->v0.19, current_pack and handoff to CP00-153/RP03.P08.M06.S18 are consistent, and all observed validations/tests pass. No correctness, hidden-write, cross-tenant, LDIP/HRX, or production-gate blockers found; only minor non-blocking observations.

findings.title: Dead code carried over from CP00-151 template (unused FAILURE_UNITS, unreachable RP03.P07 branches)
findings.details: FAILURE_UNITS is defined (22 entries) but never referenced; CP152_MICRO_PHASES uses EVIDENCE_UNITS for all three micro-phases. In domainFor() the entire `if (micro.phase_id === "RP03.P07")` block is unreachable because every CP152 micro-phase has phase_id "RP03.P08", and statusFor()'s `micro.phase_id === "RP03.P07" ? "evidence_workflow" : "evidence_packet"` ternary always resolves to "evidence_packet". This is benign residue from copying the CP00-151 catalog (which spanned P07/P08). No assertion depends on the dead paths and all status values still end in "_bound".
findings.recommendation: Remove the unused FAILURE_UNITS constant and the unreachable RP03.P07 branches in domainFor/statusFor to prevent confusion for future readers reviewing why a P08-only pack handles P07.

findings.title: Hermes evidence packet references a closeout manifest not present in the reviewed tree
findings.details: createAuditComplianceCp152HermesEvidencePacket lists "docs/closeout-packs/cp00-152/manifest.json" in evidence_refs, but that path is not present in the git status snapshot (the catalog .js itself appears as untracked, yet no cp00-152 manifest is shown). Both the CP00-152 test and validate-rp03-audit-architecture.mjs read this manifest inside try/catch and fall back to closeoutPackPlan.packs.find("CP00-152"), so validation passes whether or not the manifest exists; coverage is driven entirely by the regenerated plan. Cannot be confirmed as truly missing from the diff alone, and it may be produced at the closeout-commit step as in prior packs.
findings.recommendation: Before final closeout, confirm docs/closeout-packs/cp00-152/manifest.json is generated and committed consistent with CP00-135..CP00-151 so the asserted Hermes evidence_ref resolves to a real artifact.

findings.title: Catalog no_write_attestation is a superset of the contract attestation
findings.details: CP152_NO_WRITE_ATTESTATION (also surfaced as manifest.no_write_attestation) carries failure-domain keys not present in contract.evidence_workflow_fixture_no_write_attestation, e.g. evaluates_failure_taxonomy, executes_failure_recovery, throws_failure, executes_retry_exhaustion, executes_rollback_expectation, executes_compensation, emits_blocked_claim_receipt, emits_audit_failure_hint, emits_hermes_failure_evidence, executes_failure_unit_test/integration_smoke, materializes_failure_case_payload, materializes_claude_edge_case_prompt, records_human_escalation_note. All are false (stricter, harmless), and the architecture validator only checks the contract's own keys, so nothing fails. However the contract and code attestations no longer share an identical key set.
findings.recommendation: Either trim the catalog attestation to mirror the contract's evidence_workflow_fixture_no_write_attestation key set, or intentionally document the superset (these inherited failure-lane guarantees are reasonable for the M04 secondary-workflow terminal context). Non-blocking.

notes: No P0/P1/P2 blockers identified. Coverage (40 units), ordering, uniqueness, plan match, handoff (CP00-151->CP00-152->CP00-153, next_subphase RP03.P08.M06.S18), schema bump to v0.19, and all readiness/distribution assertions are internally consistent and confirmed by the observed test/validation runs.

No-write, hidden-write, cross-tenant, count/object-name leakage, hash-chain/append-only, and LDIP/HRX separation concerns are all satisfied: the catalog is pure descriptor generation with no data access, no event writes, and explicit false attestations enforced and tested across every row.

All three findings are P3 (non-blocking). The manifest-reference item (finding 2) is the only one touching evidence integrity; recommend a quick confirmation at closeout rather than treating it as a gate.

Reviewer could not directly inspect docs/closeout-pack-plan/closeout-pack-plan.json or the weighted ledger; coverage correctness is inferred from the passing element-by-element plan comparison in validateAuditComplianceCp152Coverage and rp03:audit:validate.
